flags.concern: Topic-editor rejected transitions: adding a distinct rejected_post_topic label requires updating build_rejected_transition's allowed action set; otherwise post_topic rejection paths raise ValueError instead of returning tool_error.
flags.evidence: Addressed all 10 open significant flags by tightening the dispatch and supporting-infrastructure spec. Key revisions: (1) Added explicit Step 3.5 extending `build_rejected_transition`'s allowed-action set at topic_editor.py:3870 to include `rejected_post_topic` — resolves the ValueError risk on collision/empty-blocks rejections (FLAG-001, correctness-1, all_locations-2). (2) Replaced the assert-empty-media plan in alias normalization with an explicit ordering rule: legacy structural guards (post_simple media check, source-count check) run BEFORE any normalization, so existing structured rejections (`post_simple_cannot_attach_media_use_post_sectioned_topic`) and their pinned tests at :305-337 keep firing unchanged (FLAG-002, correctness-2). (3) Generalized the writeback at topic_editor.py:1849-1850 to fire for `post_topic` in addition to `post_sectioned_topic`, so default-media-attached blocks reach `args` and `_summary_for_tool` (FLAG-003, all_locations-1). (4) Reversed the prior plan's decision to drop legacy `body` from legacy-alias storage: legacy alias calls now KEEP their existing storage shape (body+sections+blocks), and only NEW `post_topic` calls are blocks-only — minimizes downstream-reader risk (issue_hints). (5) Made the original-vs-normalized tool-name handoff convention explicit: `_summary_for_tool` is called with the ORIGINAL tool name and selects shape per name; telemetry labels (`post_simple`/`post_sectioned`) stay tied to the original name while the unified blocks pipeline runs internally (callers). (6) Added Step 7.2 explicitly updating the existing action-coverage test at tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py:1380-1437 to include `post_topic` and `rejected_post_topic` (scope). Also added two new tests: one for `rejected_post_topic` collision returning tool_error (verifies the helper extension), and one pinning the post_simple media-rejection ordering. Success criteria updated to reflect the no-drop-body decision for aliases and to add a criterion for `build_rejected_transition` accepting `rejected_post_topic` without raising.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the remaining model-visible steering surfaces in the repository. The plan still does not mention the auto-shortlisted topic summary at src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:2154-2160, which includes `post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic/update sources, or discard_topic`; because those summaries are included in active_topics payloads, FLAG-005 remains open.
flags.evidence: Addressed the two remaining real flag clusters. (1) FLAG-004 / correctness / callers: split the blocks-required guard into separate per-tool branches at topic_editor.py:1893-1905. `post_topic` now requires `has_blocks` UNCONDITIONALLY — a stray non-empty `sections` field in args no longer satisfies the contract. Belt-and-braces with `blocks: {minItems: 1}` in the schema entry. Added a new regression test `test_post_topic_rejects_empty_blocks_even_with_stray_sections`. `post_sectioned_topic`'s legacy `or` semantics (`not has_sections and not has_blocks`) are preserved verbatim. (2) FLAG-005 / issue_hints / scope / all_locations: added Step 5 to update the auto-shortlist suggested-actions string at topic_editor.py:2154-2160, replacing the literal `post_sectioned_topic` token with `post_topic` so the model is not steered to the legacy tool from inside `active_topics` payloads. Added this location to the Step 1 audit list explicitly and added a regression test `test_auto_shortlist_suggested_actions_uses_post_topic_not_post_sectioned_topic`. Renumbered subsequent steps (system prompt rewrite is now Step 6, publisher verification Step 7, tests Step 8, validation Step 9). The other two flag IDs (`callers`, `correctness`, `all_locations`) are duplicates of FLAG-004/FLAG-005 and are addressed by the same fixes. Recurring critiques for criteria 16/17 (subjective_judgment, inspect_runtime_ui) are inherent verification-capability requirements, not plan defects, so they remain as `should`/`info`.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked rejection flow for create-topic tools. _reject_create_tool delegates to build_rejected_transition, and build_rejected_transition currently only accepts rejected_post_simple, rejected_post_sectioned, and rejected_watch at src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:3859-3871. The plan says to add post_topic to the rejected-action map 'similarly' to accepted action_by_tool; if that becomes rejected_post_topic, any post_topic rejection such as topic_collision or post_topic_requires_blocks will raise ValueError unless build_rejected_transition is also extended or post_topic reuses an existing rejected action label.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the current post_simple_topic media guard at src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:1858-1880. The plan's proposed _normalize_post_topic_call says to synthesize an intro block for post_simple_topic and 'assert empty' legacy media, while also saying the legacy media/source-count guards remain intact. If the normalizer runs before those guards, an invalid post_simple_topic media call would crash or assert instead of returning the current structured rejection post_simple_cannot_attach_media_use_post_sectioned_topic, so the plan needs to specify graceful normalization or validation ordering.

flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the block normalization/default-media branch at src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:1844-1850. It currently writes normalized/default-media blocks back into args only when call['name'] == 'post_sectioned_topic'. The plan says validation and persistence should deal with the unified blocks shape, but its specific line-by-line edits do not mention changing this condition for post_topic; if missed, post_topic summaries produced by _summary_for_tool could store unnormalized blocks and lose automatically attached default media_refs even though validation/transition payloads saw normalized_blocks.
